passwordPolicy: passwordHistory(3) and length(8) and notEmail(undefined) and notUsername(undefined) and notContainsUsername(undefined) and specialChars(1) and upperCase(1) and lowerCase(1) and digits(1) and maxLength(64)

en.termsText:
1.	Purpose & Scope
This document establishes a transparent, trust-based framework for our community-hosted digital services. It is not a legally binding corporate contract, but a mutual agreement to ensure digital sovereignty, privacy, and shared responsibility among friends and peers.
2.	Roles & Responsibilities
-	The Host: Maintains the physical hardware, configures the server infrastructure, manages software updates, and monitors basic system health. The Host acts in good faith to keep services secure and operational but offers no commercial guarantees.
-	The Members: Granted access to the hosted services for personal, non-commercial use. Members agree to use services responsibly, respect shared bandwidth/storage caps, and protect their account credentials.
3.	Data Access & Privacy Commitments
By definition, self-hosting gives the Host root administrative access to the underlying infrastructure.
-	The Host can technically view system logs, database contents, unencrypted files, and user metadata (such as login times and IP addresses).
-	The Host explicitly commits to a policy of non-interference. The Host will never read, copy, share, or analyze Member data unless explicitly requested by the Member for troubleshooting. System logs will only be used to diagnose performance or security issues and will auto-delete after 14 days.
4.	Service Availability & Support
-	This is a hobbyist, best-effort setup. Members should expect occasional downtime for maintenance, power outages, or internet disruptions.
-	While the Host configures automated backups for disaster recovery, the Host is not liable for data loss. Members are strongly encouraged to keep independent backups of irreplaceable personal data.
5.	Dispute Resolution
If a disagreement arises regarding resource usage, data privacy, or behavior:
1.	The affected parties will first attempt to resolve the issue through direct, informal conversation.
2.	The Host retains the final right to temporarily suspend an account if a Member’s actions actively threaten the security or stability of the entire server.
6.	Offboarding & Termination
-	A Member can leave the arrangement at any time. Upon request, the Host will provide a 30-day window for the Member to export their data. After 30 days, the Host will permanently and securely delete the Member’s account and all associated data.
-	If the Host decides to stop running the server, they must give all Members at least 30 days' notice. During this period, the Host will assist Members in exporting their data or transitioning to an alternative hosting solution.

clientScopes.name: acr
clientScopes.description: OpenID Connect scope for add acr (authentication context class reference) to the token

clientScopes.name: web-origins
clientScopes.description: OpenID Connect scope for add allowed web origins to the access token

clientScopes.name: basic
clientScopes.description: OpenID Connect scope for add all basic claims to the token

clientScopes.name: roles
clientScopes.description: OpenID Connect scope for add user roles to the access token

clientScopes.name: organization
clientScopes.description: Additional claims about the organization a subject belongs to

browserSecurityHeaders.contentSecurityPolicy: frame-src 'self' https:; frame-ancestors 'self' https:; object-src 'none'; upgrade-insecure-requests; block-all-mixed-content;
